summary: Verified the backend Docker-build fix for the missing `emergentintegrations` package on GH Actions. Static check: /app/backend/Dockerfile lines 20-22 now use `pip install -r requirements.txt --extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/` тАФ confirmed present verbatim. Build simulation (the crux of this iteration): the container has Emergent's global pip config (`/root/.config/pip/pip.conf` тЖТ `global.extra-index-url=https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/`), so a naive test masks the failure. To faithfully simulate the GH Actions runner (which has NO such config), I ran (a) `PIP_CONFIG_FILE=/dev/null pip install --isolated --index-url https://pypi.org/simple/ emergentintegrations==0.2.0` тЖТ FAILED with `ERROR: No matching distribution found for emergentintegrations==0.2.0` (this reproduces the exact CI failure); (b) `PIP_CONFIG_FILE=/dev/null pip install --isolated --index-url https://pypi.org/simple/ --extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/ -r /app/backend/requirements.txt` тЖТ exit 0, all 30 requirements installed, `emergentintegrations 0.2.0` present in `pip list` alongside fastapi 0.110.1, bcrypt 4.1.3, motor 3.3.1, reportlab 5.0.0. This is what will happen on the GH Actions runner with the fixed Dockerfile. Regression smoke: admin@dental.com/admin123 login тЖТ 200 (httpOnly cookies), GET /api/auth/me тЖТ 200, POST /api/generate with D3330/tooth 19/necrotic pulp тЖТ 200, procedure_name='Endodontic therapy, molar tooth', short_narrative 32 words D3330-appropriate, long_narrative 109 words with periapical radiolucency + necrotic pulp findings, radiograph requirements populated (PA pre/working/post-op + CBCT recommended). UI smoke: login page тЖТ dashboard with 'Welcome back' toast, Single/Visit packet/History/Appeals nav intact, procedure form renders. Frontend Dockerfile fix from iter 12 (`COPY yarn.lock* ./` glob + `yarn install` without `--frozen-lockfile`) is unchanged and still in place. No live-deployment regression from the Dockerfile change (as expected тАФ dev uses the pre-baked venv, not Docker).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend Dockerfile fix is minimal and correct: passing `--extra-index-url` on the command line keeps PyPI as the primary index (fast, no dependency-confusion risk) and only falls through to Emergent's CloudFront index for `emergentintegrations` тАФ which is unavailable on PyPI. Matches the documented emergentintegrations install pattern from integration_playbook_expert_v2.

Confirmed reproduction of the original failure mode: without the extra-index-url and with a clean pip config, `pip install emergentintegrations==0.2.0` returns `ERROR: No matching distribution found` тАФ exactly the silent-failure the user reported on GH Actions. The fix resolves it.

Consider also pinning `--index-url https://pypi.org/simple/` explicitly in the Dockerfile for defense-in-depth (guarantees primary source even if a future base image adds a different default index). Optional hardening, not required for the current fix.

The Docker daemon is not available in this preview container, so the actual multi-arch `docker buildx` step cannot be exercised end-to-end here тАФ but the pip resolution simulation is the strictly harder subset of what the Docker build does, and it succeeded from a completely isolated environment. The next `git push` should now produce a `backend` package on GHCR alongside `frontend`.

success_rate.backend: 100% (isolated pip install with --extra-index-url succeeds; regression smoke POST /api/generate D3330 тЖТ 200 with correct narratives)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login + dashboard render on live preview; no changes vs iter 12)

seed_data_creation: None (used existing seeded admin; D3330 narrative generated with save_to_history=false so no test data persisted)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 verified the Dockerfile fix for the missing emergentintegrations package (the reason backend image did not publish to GHCR while frontend did). Key insight: this container has a GLOBAL pip config at /root/.config/pip/pip.conf that silently supplies the extra-index-url, so any pip test MUST use `PIP_CONFIG_FILE=/dev/null pip install --isolated ...` to faithfully simulate GH Actions. Confirmed reproduction of the failure without extra-index-url, confirmed success with it. Docker daemon still unavailable in this environment тАФ final CI validation happens on the user's next push. Frontend Dockerfile fix from iter 12 is unchanged and still valid. No open issues.
rca of the issue: Root cause: `/app/backend/requirements.txt` line 28 pins `emergentintegrations==0.2.0`, which is Emergent's private library hosted on https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/ and is NOT published to PyPI. The previous backend Dockerfile did a plain `pip install -r requirements.txt` which only queries PyPI by default. On the GH Actions runner (which does not have Emergent's global pip config), pip could not find the package and the backend build job failed with `No matching distribution found for emergentintegrations==0.2.0`. Because the frontend and backend matrix jobs are independent in the workflow, only the frontend package appeared on GHCR тАФ matching the user's report exactly. Fix (verified in this iteration): add `--extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/` to the pip install invocation in /app/backend/Dockerfile line 22. Reproduction proof: `PIP_CONFIG_FILE=/dev/null pip install --isolated --index-url https://pypi.org/simple/ emergentintegrations==0.2.0` тЖТ `ERROR: No matching distribution found` (broken state). `PIP_CONFIG_FILE=/dev/null pip install --isolated --index-url https://pypi.org/simple/ --extra-index-url https://d33sy5i8bnduwe.cloudfront.net/simple/ -r /app/backend/requirements.txt` тЖТ exit 0, emergentintegrations-0.2.0 installed (fixed state). Mitigation: the Dockerfile change is committed at /app/backend/Dockerfile lines 20-22 and will take effect on the user's next `git push` to the tracked branch.
